_risk_level_doc: Security-critical privilege escalation: a path-scoped /allow grant silently becomes a global Write grant due to three co-required bugs. CRITICAL-2 causes no sentinel to be written for any filename with an extension. CRITICAL-1 produces a bare wildcard even for bare /allow Write /path syntax. ORIGINAL bug produces wildcard even when IS_REGEX=false and rest is populated. All affect the hook infrastructure controlling Write-tool permissions.

requirement.original: Fix schema mismatch between the /allow sentinel writer and the Write-guard matcher. Writer generates {"op":"Write","args_contain":["/path"]} — no "target" field. Matcher checks entry.get("target") and treats absent target as wildcard, granting Write to any file. Fix: writer must emit "target" field for Write ops; matcher must validate target strictly.
requirement.clarified: Three co-required bugs prevent any path-scoped Write sentinel from being produced or matched via the real /allow user path. CRITICAL-2: _looks_regex() at line 82-84 classifies any dotted filename as regex → IS_REGEX=true → sentinel write skipped entirely. CRITICAL-1: bare '/allow Write /path' treats path as comment → sentinel emits bare wildcard {'op':'Write'}. ORIGINAL: even when IS_REGEX=false and rest is populated, writer emits args_contain not target → matcher wildcards. Fix: (1) force is_regex=False for flag_pattern (--tool flag is explicit literal); (2) special-case bare 'Write <path>' to emit scoped sentinel; (3) writer emits 'target' field for Write ops; (4) matcher uses key-presence branching, fail-closed.
requirement.what: Fix three upstream bugs in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh plus the matcher schema mismatch in allowlist.py to make path-scoped Write grants functional for real-world filenames.
requirement.why: Privilege escalation: a user granting write access to a single file inadvertently grants unrestricted Write access to the entire filesystem. For filenames with extensions (the majority of real cases), no sentinel is produced at all — the escalation is a silent no-op, not a grant.
where: hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh (CRITICAL-2: lines 82-88; CRITICAL-1: lines 90-97; ORIGINAL: lines 199-204)

included: hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh — fix IS_REGEX override (CRITICAL-2), fix bare Write<path> parsing (CRITICAL-1), fix writer to emit target field (ORIGINAL)
hooks/lib/allowlist.py — fix matcher key-presence logic, update stale docstring
excluded: hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh — implicitly fixed by matcher change, no direct edits
hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py — implicitly fixed by matcher change, no direct edits
match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command — bash semantics are correct and unchanged
Retroactive invalidation of in-flight sentinels (expire by TTL in 300 s)
Stale legacy grant cleanup after sentinel consume (separate cycle, Codex finding 6)
Regex-skip stale sentinel suppression (separate cycle, Codex finding 5)
Compound-Bash sentinel matching (separate security cycle, Codex finding 10)

success_criteria: /allow --tool "Write /tmp/file.json" produces a sentinel with target=/tmp/file.json (not args_contain, not missing)
/allow Write /tmp/file.json produces a sentinel with target=/tmp/file.json (not bare wildcard)
match_sentinel_grant_for_write returns non-None only when target_path exactly equals the granted path
Legacy args_contain sentinel shape allows exactly the named path and denies all others (exact equality, no substring)
Bare {op:Write} sentinel (intentional wildcard) continues to match any path
posttool consume correctly fires for new target-schema sentinel
Exploit-shape regression test passes: args_contain sentinel denies /other/path and /specific/path-suffix
constraints: Field name must be 'target' not 'target_path' (per allowlist.py:494 schema comment)
Non-Write ops must retain existing args_contain behavior in the writer
Matcher changes must not break existing tests at TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite
Path arguments must be absolute; writer emits verbatim; matcher compares by exact string equality
CRITICAL-2 fix (is_regex=False for flag_pattern) and ORIGINAL writer fix (target field) are atomic — must land together

setup.reason: non-UI -- hook; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths (pipeline steps, API endpoints). These are PreToolUse/PostToolUse hook files invoked by the Claude Code harness, not by browser sessions.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Regex-skip exit does not clear stale sentinel for the same TASK_ID. A later /allow re:... grant with an existing stale sentinel can cause CF-1 to suppress the legacy regex grant. Requires stale-sentinel cleanup on IS_REGEX=true exit.

After sentinel is consumed on Write, the stale legacy pattern-grant file /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-<sid>.json may remain. Pattern 'Write /tmp/file.json' in the legacy file could later match a Bash command by substring (non-Bash exact-only branch prevents this for tool-name match, but Bash branch uses substr_only). Codex finding 6. Separate cycle.

match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command allows the entire compound Bash command if any single subcommand matches the sentinel grant entry. A grant for 'echo ok' could authorize 'dangerous_command; echo ok'. Codex finding 10. Separate security cycle.

evidence.observed: /allow --tool "Write /some/path" produces sentinel with args_contain=["/some/path"] but NO target field (or no sentinel at all for dotted filenames); matcher treats absent target as wildcard and grants Write to ANY file. Bare /allow Write /path produces wildcard sentinel.

measured.value: CRITICAL-2: /allow --tool "Write /tmp/allowed.json" → is_regex=true → sentinel write SKIPPED → no sentinel produced. CRITICAL-1: /allow Write /tmp/allowed.json → pattern='Write', comment='/tmp/allowed.json' → sentinel {op:Write} bare wildcard. ORIGINAL: when IS_REGEX=false and dotless path: entry={'op':op}; if rest: entry['args_contain']=[rest] — no 'target' key.

expected.value: entry = {'op': 'Write', 'target': '/some/path'} — target field required for path-scoped grant; sentinel must be written for any /allow --tool "Write /path" regardless of dots in filename
expected.source: hooks/lib/allowlist.py:484-524 match_sentinel_grant_for_write docstring + schema comment at line 494; QA runtime verification

evidence.gap: Three co-required bugs: (1) _looks_regex() classifies dotted paths as regex → IS_REGEX=true → sentinel never written for .json/.py/.sh etc; (2) bare Write <path> syntax discards path as comment → wildcard sentinel; (3) writer never emits 'target' → matcher's entry_target is None → wildcard match. All three must be fixed.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: User grants Write access to a single file via /allow --tool "Write /path" but the hook allows Write to any file — or produces no grant at all for filenames with extensions.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Three co-required bugs: (CRITICAL-2) _looks_regex() at line 82-84 includes '.' in its metacharacter set; flag_pattern 'Write /tmp/file.json' contains '.', so is_regex=True is set even for --tool, causing the sentinel write to be skipped at line 180-182. (CRITICAL-1) Bare '/allow Write /path' argument parsing treats the path as a comment (bare[1:]), not as a path argument, so the sentinel writer receives pattern='Write' with no rest and emits a bare wildcard {'op':'Write'}. (ORIGINAL) Even when IS_REGEX=false and rest is populated, the writer emits args_contain instead of target; the matcher checks only 'target' and wildcards on absent key.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — mismatch appears to have been present since match_sentinel_grant_for_write was introduced in cycle 20260522-080646; CRITICAL-2 and CRITICAL-1 appear to predate that cycle
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: CRITICAL-2: _looks_regex() was designed for the legacy /allow pattern (which can be a regex) — it was not adjusted when the --tool flag was added as an explicit literal channel. CRITICAL-1: the bare token parser correctly handles 'pattern + comment' but did not special-case 'Write <path>' as a scoped grant. ORIGINAL: the sentinel writer bootstrapped from the legacy pattern-string format using args_contain; the matcher was designed for the target field; the two were never synchronized.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: For the vast majority of real-world file paths (those with extensions like .json, .py, .sh), no sentinel is written at all (CRITICAL-2). For the natural bare syntax, a wildcard is written instead of a scoped grant (CRITICAL-1). Even for the rare dotless path via --tool, the sentinel is written with the wrong field name and the matcher wildcards (ORIGINAL).
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Present since match_sentinel_grant_for_write was introduced in cycle 20260522-080646. CRITICAL-2 and CRITICAL-1 are pre-existing behaviors of the /allow parser that predated Write-sentinel logic.
observations: /allow --tool "Write /tmp/allowed.json" → IS_REGEX=true → sentinel write skipped → no sentinel produced (observed: QA live runtime execution)
/allow Write /tmp/allowed.json → pattern='Write', comment='/tmp/allowed.json' → sentinel {'op':'Write'} bare wildcard (observed: QA live runtime execution)
When IS_REGEX=false and dotless path: entry={'op':op}; if rest: entry['args_contain']=[rest] — no 'target' key (inferred: read userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:199-204)
Matcher: entry.get('target') → None → 'entry_target is None' → True → wildcard match (inferred: read allowlist.py:521-523)
_looks_regex() character class includes '.': r'[\^$\\.*+?\[\]\(\){}|]' (inferred: read line 84)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: complete (QA provided live runtime evidence for CRITICAL-1 and CRITICAL-2; code read confirms ORIGINAL bug)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: QA runtime: (1) /allow --tool "Write /tmp/allowed.json" → stdout shows IS_REGEX=true → sentinel MISSING. (2) /allow Write /tmp/allowed.json → stdout shows pattern='Write' comment='/tmp/allowed.json' → sentinel {'op':'Write'} bare wildcard. Code read confirms _looks_regex() at line 84 includes '.' in metacharacter set; flag_pattern branch at lines 86-88 calls _looks_regex() for flag_pattern too.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: Not a regression complaint — no 'used to work' signal; bugs present since match_sentinel_grant_for_write was introduced

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: sentinel produced has args_contain=["/some/path"] but NO target field
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: AC5 — exploit-shape regression: {op:Write,args_contain:["/specific/path"]} matches only /specific/path and denies /other/path and /specific/path-suffix

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: matcher then sees no target and grants Write access to ANY file
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: AC2 — scoped grant blocks any file other than the named one

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: writer MUST generate a 'target' field for Write operations (not just args_contain)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: AC1 + AC7 — sentinel with target field allows the named file exactly; end-to-end stack verified

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: matcher MUST validate target strictly (reject when target absent and file path does not match args_contain)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: AC4 — legacy args_contain exact equality; AC6 — intentional bare wildcard preserved

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: Posttool correctly consumes the grant after Write use
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: AC3 — posttool unlinks sentinel after Write to named file
AC8 — bare form must produce scoped sentinel with target field

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: _looks_regex() classifies dotted path as regex, skips sentinel write (QA CRITICAL-2)
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: AC7 — full end-to-end test proves sentinel written for dotted filename via --tool

component_chain.role: Sentinel writer — parses /allow prompt, decides IS_REGEX, writes legacy grant and sentinel at /tmp/claude-grants/<task_id>.json. Three bugs here: _looks_regex() override (CRITICAL-2, lines 82-88), bare Write <path> comment-strip (CRITICAL-1, lines 90-97), wrong field name in writer (ORIGINAL, lines 199-204).
component_chain.evidence: Read hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh full file; QA live runtime execution

component_chain.role: match_sentinel_grant_for_write — validates Write target against sentinel entry. Bug here: entry.get('target') is None wildcards instead of key-presence check (ORIGINAL, lines 521-523). Stale docstring at lines 488-492.
Pretool guard — calls match_sentinel_grant_for_write; emits match/valid_no_match/none. Implicitly fixed by matcher fix; no direct edits needed.
Posttool consume — calls match_sentinel_grant_for_write and consume_sentinel_grant_on_terminal_result. Implicitly fixed by matcher fix; no direct edits needed.

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Ensures only Write-op entries are considered by match_sentinel_grant_for_write
CF-1 invariant: suppresses legacy grant fallback when a valid but non-matching sentinel exists (overwrite only)

context.recent_commits: aea75d9 chore(docs): ship cycle 20260522-000000 close-report and QA verification; 5771880 feat(spec): commit spec-20260520-221059 and split views to git (arch-2); af96c61 fix(infra): M1-M4 gitignore exceptions, install mirror, uncapped_delta (cycle 20260522-000000)

dependencies.hooks/lib/allowlist.py: stdlib only (json, fcntl, os, re, signal, sys, time, pathlib); Python 3.12+

development_approach.strategy: Four surgical changes in dependency order: (1) In userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh Python parser, set is_regex=False unconditionally for flag_pattern (CRITICAL-2 fix). (2) In userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh Python parser, special-case bare 'Write <path>' to extract the path as part of the pattern (CRITICAL-1 fix). (3) In the sentinel writer block, replace args_contain with target for Write ops (ORIGINAL fix). (4) In match_sentinel_grant_for_write, replace entry.get('target') is None wildcard with key-presence branching, fail-closed on malformed entries (ORIGINAL matcher fix). Then add AC4/AC5/AC7/AC8 regression tests.
development_approach.validation_approach: Run existing TestMatchSentinelGrantForWrite test class to confirm no regressions; add AC4/AC5/AC7/AC8 test cases; AC7 and AC8 invoke userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh as subprocess with a JSON payload and verify the sentinel file contents, then call match_sentinel_grant_for_write against the produced sentinel.

attempts.proposed_solution: Fix writer to emit 'target' instead of 'args_contain' for Write ops; fix matcher key-presence logic
attempts.actual_change: Spec only — no dev change landed
attempts.outcome: QA rejected: two critical upstream writer-path bugs (CRITICAL-1: bare /allow Write /path produces wildcard; CRITICAL-2: _looks_regex() classifies dotted filenames as regex → sentinel write skipped) make the writer fix unreachable for any filename with an extension. ACs were matcher-only and could only pass with hand-crafted sentinels.

novelty_check.rationale: Iteration 1 targeted only L3 (sentinel schema field). This iteration targets L4 (argument parsing logic: IS_REGEX decision, bare Write<path> token classification) in addition to L3. L4 fixes are prerequisite to the L3 writer fix being reachable.

reference_source.description: Source files read directly in this session; schema documented in allowlist.py module docstring

research_findings.reason: Straightforward data-schema mismatch bug with clear root cause identified by code read; no external research needed.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (iteration 2) independently verified CRITICAL-1 and CRITICAL-2 via live runtime execution and identified 10 findings. Key findings: (1) --tool => is_regex=False is correct but docs must be updated; (2) CRITICAL coupling: fixing CRITICAL-2 alone still produces wildcard unless writer fix (target field) also lands; (3) Option A (special-case bare Write <path>) is safer than documentation-only Option B; (4) Unquoted --tool Write /path (no quotes) still produces wildcard — must document; (5) Regex-skip can leave stale sentinel (out of scope); (6) Sentinel consume may leave stale legacy grant (out of scope); (7) AC7 must include full subprocess verification; (8) Path equality policy needed; (9) No regex bypass risk from --tool literal (observation only); (10) Compound-Bash sentinel matching (out of scope, security relevant).

findings.issue: --tool => is_regex=False is correct but docs and hook comments still say regex is auto-detected for --tool
findings.proposed_fix: Set is_regex=False in flag_pattern is not None branch; update commands/allow.md description
findings.rationale: Encoded in M1 and Technical Hints. Doc update added to scope.

findings.issue: CRITICAL coupling: fixing CRITICAL-2 alone (is_regex=False) without writer fix (target field) produces a worse state — IS_REGEX=false now reaches writer, which still emits args_contain, which matcher wildcards
findings.proposed_fix: Both CRITICAL-2 fix and writer fix (target field) must be atomic — do not land one without the other
findings.rationale: Added as constraint: 'CRITICAL-2 fix and ORIGINAL writer fix are atomic — must land together.'

findings.issue: For CRITICAL-1, Option A (special-case bare Write <path>) is safer than Option B (documentation only)
findings.proposed_fix: If bare[0]=='Write' and len(bare)>=2, treat bare[1] as path, bare[2:] as comment
findings.rationale: Documentation alone does not prevent users from typing the unsafe form. Option A special-casing is encoded in M2 and Technical Hints.

findings.issue: Unquoted '/allow --tool Write /path' (no quotes) still produces wildcard — --tool consumes only 'Write' as pattern, '/path' lands in bare as comment
findings.proposed_fix: Document clearly that quoted form '/allow --tool "Write /path"' is required for scoped grants via --tool flag
findings.rationale: Added as Edge Case 5 (out of scope for this cycle but documented).

findings.issue: Regex-skip path (IS_REGEX=true, exit 0) does not clear stale sentinel for same TASK_ID — can cause CF-1 to suppress legacy regex grant
findings.proposed_fix: When IS_REGEX=true, delete/replace any existing sentinel for same TASK_ID before exiting
findings.rationale: Out of scope for this cycle — separate concern from Write sentinel path. Recorded in out_of_scope_observations.

findings.issue: After Write sentinel is consumed, stale legacy pattern-grant file may remain and match later Bash command by substring
findings.proposed_fix: When Write sentinel match is consumed, also unlink legacy /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-<sid>.json
findings.rationale: Out of scope for this cycle. Recorded in out_of_scope_observations.

findings.issue: AC7 end-to-end requires full subprocess ACs including pretool-write-guard.sh allow/deny and posttool sentinel removal
findings.proposed_fix: Add full subprocess ACs verifying userprompt writes sentinel with target, matcher allows/denies correctly
findings.rationale: AC7 and AC8 now include full subprocess verification of the writer→sentinel→matcher stack.

findings.issue: Path equality policy undefined — relative paths, ~, symlinks could cause surprising deny/allow
findings.proposed_fix: Require absolute paths for scoped Write grants; emit verbatim, compare by exact string equality
findings.rationale: Added as constraint and Edge Case 7 (path equality policy).

findings.issue: No regex-ReDoS bypass from literal --tool if target is exact — abuse risk is wildcard/substring fallout, not regex evaluation
findings.rationale: Question 5 from BA prompt. Observation only — confirms BA's assessment.

findings.issue: match_sentinel_grant_for_bash_command allows entire compound Bash command if any single subcommand matches grant
findings.proposed_fix: For Bash sentinel grants, require every subcommand to be structurally covered
findings.rationale: Out of scope for this cycle — separate security concern from Write sentinel path. Recorded in out_of_scope_observations with security_relevant=true.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Findings 1-4 and 7-8 incorporated into revised spec (M1-M8, constraints, edge cases, AC7/AC8). Findings 5, 6, 10 recorded in out_of_scope_observations. Finding 9 observation-only acknowledged.
